commands.review_summary:
The PR adds shared bounded response readers to OpenAI ChatGPT/Codex OAuth token exchange and refresh response parsing.

PR surface: Source +19. Total +19 across 1 file.

Reproducibility: Source-level yes, live replay no. A large token response reaches `postTokenForm` and is consumed by `response.arrayBuffer()` before any new bounded reader in this PR runs.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] Human/contributor follow-up remains because the patch needs a first-read repair and changed-path real behavior proof before merge; automation cannot supply the contributor proof gate.

target.title: fix(openai): bound OAuth token response reads to prevent OOM
target.body:
## What Problem This Solves
Replace unbounded response.text() in error paths and response.json() in success paths with readResponseWithLimit and readProviderJsonResponse (16 MiB cap) in OpenAI Codex OAuth token exchange and token refresh functions.

## Evidence

**Real environment tested**: Linux, Node 22, OpenClaw main @ 87db23e543

**Proof evidence**:
```
=== error path (readResponseWithLimit) ===
  chunks:17 cancelled:true PASS
=== success path (readProviderJsonResponse) ===
  chunks:17 cancelled:true PASS

PASS: both OpenAI OAuth paths bounded at 16 MiB
```

**All existing tests pass**: 10/10 in openai-chatgpt-oauth-flow.runtime.test.ts.

## Risk
Low. The 16 MiB cap matches the codebase convention. Error paths use readResponseWithLimit + .catch(() => "") to preserve the existing graceful degradation pattern.
target.author: cxbasdev
